skill.name: firebase-app-hosting-basics
skill.description: Deploy and manage Next.js, Angular, and other full-stack web apps with Firebase App Hosting.
skill.summary: Guide Firebase App Hosting setup, configuration, deployment, secrets, and local emulation.
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged a critical combination of commands, network references, and secret terms, but review found documentation-only Markdown with no executable code, obfuscation, prompt injection, or data exfiltration. Most static hits are false positives from Markdown backticks, prose, or official Firebase examples. Risk remains medium because the skill guides agents through Firebase CLI commands that can deploy, delete, or change cloud resources and manage secrets.

medium_findings.title: Firebase CLI Commands Can Modify Cloud Resources
medium_findings.description: The skill documents Firebase CLI commands for deployment, backend deletion, rollout creation, backend creation, and secret access. These are legitimate App Hosting workflows, but they can change or delete Firebase resources if run without clear user approval.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are plainly documented and include deploy, delete, create, and rollout operations. They are legitimate Firebase commands, but the operational impact is real.

medium_findings.title: Secret Handling Workflow Requires User Confirmation
medium_findings.description: The skill includes App Hosting secret commands and examples using API_KEY values. This is expected for Firebase App Hosting, but agents must avoid exposing secret values in logs, commits, prompts, or generated files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files clearly describe secret creation and local secret overrides. The guidance is not malicious, but mishandling these workflows could expose credentials.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Command Alerts Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer labeled many Markdown backtick snippets as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The reviewed files contain documentation and fenced examples, not executable Ruby code or dynamic shell evaluation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown headings, inline code, or fenced examples. No file contains Ruby code, eval behavior, or shell interpolation logic.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Lack Supporting Evidence
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm blockers, but the cited lines are prose or configuration guidance. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, insecure cipher use, or custom cryptography in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines do not contain cryptographic APIs or algorithm names. They describe Firebase App Hosting and local emulator configuration.

low_findings.title: Network Findings Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to the official Firebase Console billing page, and the configuration line flagged as network access is an environment availability value. No evidence found of outbound HTTP requests or data transmission code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only real URL is an official Firebase Console link for plan setup. The other cited line is the word RUNTIME in a YAML example, not a Python HTTP library.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented External CLI Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs agents to use npx firebase-tools commands. These commands are expected for deployment, but they should be treated as user-approved external operations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command strings are explicit and operational. They are not hidden or malicious, but they execute external tooling.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented Secret Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: The references include API_KEY examples and secret configuration fields. This is normal App Hosting documentation, but it requires careful handling of credential values.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The secret examples are directly visible in configuration snippets. The risk depends on user behavior rather than malicious skill code.

content.value_statement: Firebase App Hosting setup has several deployment, secret, and emulator decisions. This skill guides agents through App Hosting configuration, Firebase CLI workflows, and local testing steps.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to choose Firebase Hosting or Firebase App Hosting.
Guides apphosting.yaml and firebase.json configuration for App Hosting.
Lists Firebase CLI commands for backends, rollouts, secrets, and deployment.
Describes local App Hosting emulation with Firebase Local Emulator Suite.
Documents secret setup and access grants through Firebase CLI workflows.
limitations: Does not replace Firebase Console permissions or billing requirements.
Requires Firebase CLI access and a valid Firebase project.
Does not validate framework-specific application code automatically.
Cloud resource changes still require user review before execution.

use_cases.title: Prepare a New App Hosting Deployment
use_cases.description: Configure a full-stack web app for Firebase App Hosting with the required files and deployment commands.

use_cases.title: Manage App Hosting Secrets
use_cases.description: Set up secret-backed environment variables and grant App Hosting service access safely.

use_cases.title: Test App Hosting Locally
use_cases.description: Use the Firebase Local Emulator Suite to verify App Hosting behavior before deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Hosting Mode
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me decide whether this project should use Firebase Hosting or Firebase App Hosting. Ask for missing framework and rendering details first.

prompt_templates.title: Create App Hosting Config
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my project structure and propose the Firebase App Hosting configuration I need. Include required files and explain each setting.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Secrets Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me move these environment variables into Firebase App Hosting secrets. Identify which values are safe literals and which need Secret Manager.

prompt_templates.title: Design CI/CD Rollout Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Firebase App Hosting deployment workflow for GitHub-connected rollouts. Include rollback checks and commands that need explicit approval.

output_examples.input: I have a Next.js app with server-side rendering and API routes.
output_examples.output: Recommendation: use Firebase App Hosting. The response explains required configuration, secret handling, deployment prerequisites, and local emulator testing.

output_examples.input: I need to add an API key to App Hosting without committing it.
output_examples.output: The skill outlines a Secret Manager workflow, access grant checks, and a reminder to keep local emulator overrides out of source control.

output_examples.input: I want to deploy from GitHub instead of running manual deploys.
output_examples.output: The skill describes backend creation, rollout behavior, branch selection, and approval points for production changes.

best_practices: Confirm the Firebase project and billing plan before deployment commands run.
Use Secret Manager for sensitive values instead of committed configuration files.
Run the App Hosting emulator before production deployment when behavior is uncertain.
anti_patterns: Do not run backend delete or rollout commands without explicit user approval.
Do not place real API keys in committed apphosting.emulator.yaml files.
Do not use App Hosting for simple static sites that Firebase Hosting can serve directly.

faq.question: What frameworks does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets full-stack web frameworks such as Next.js and Angular that can use Firebase App Hosting.

faq.question: Does App Hosting require a paid Firebase plan?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill notes that Firebase App Hosting requires the Firebase Blaze pricing plan.

faq.question: Can this skill deploy my app automatically?
faq.answer: It provides deployment commands and guidance. The user still controls credentials, project selection, and command execution.

faq.question: Does this skill handle secrets safely?
faq.answer: It recommends Firebase App Hosting secrets and Secret Manager workflows. Users must still avoid sharing secret values in prompts or files.

faq.question: Can I test App Hosting before deployment?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents local testing with Firebase Local Emulator Suite for App Hosting.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for production projects?
faq.answer: It is suitable with review. Production commands can modify cloud resources, so users should approve each command before execution.
